stem: A tree casts a shadow that is 60 feet long on level ground. From the tip of the shadow, the angle of elevation to the top of the tree is 53 degrees. Given that tan 53° ≈ 4/3, sin 53° ≈ 0.8, and cos 53° ≈ 0.6, what is the height of the tree, in feet?
explanation: The tree height is the side opposite the 53° angle and the shadow is the side adjacent to it. Using the tangent ratio, tan 53° = opposite/adjacent = height/60. So height = 60 × (4/3) = 80 feet.

distractorRationale.B: Used sin 53° instead of tan 53° to find the height: 60 × 0.8 = 48.
distractorRationale.C: Used cos 53° instead of tan 53° to find the height: 60 × 0.6 = 36.
distractorRationale.D: Inverted the tangent ratio, dividing by tan instead of multiplying: 60 × (3/4) = 45.
